rag_rules: Attach AGREE text inside an `annex agree {** ... **};` block on the correct AADL owner.
Keep assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations in component type scope when they refer only to that component's visible features.
Keep implementation-only bindings in implementation scope, and place implementation-scoped AGREE content after the subcomponent or connection declarations it depends on.
Do not fuse type-scoped contracts that reference raw subcomponent port paths unless those values are exposed through valid owner-visible symbols.
generic_rules: Preserve the existing architecture and edit only the minimal owner body needed for the annex insertion.
If the chosen owner already has an AGREE annex, extend that owner-scoped annex instead of creating conflicting duplicate structure.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Maintain exact annex framing and AADL delimiters: `annex agree {** ... **};`.
anti_patterns: Do not choose scope based only on which element is being edited; match scope to the declarations and symbols used by the AGREE content.
Do not move, rename, or rewrite unrelated features, subcomponents, connections, or properties while inserting AGREE text.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
